meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve Debugging Woes
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Use this SaaS starter kit checklist to master debugging with Antigravity and Claude Code. Perfect for beginner AI builders.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't about trial and error; it's about systematic isolation. By leveraging a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you inherit pre-configured error handling and logging. Using Claude Code for root-cause analysis and Antigravity for architectural visualization, you transform opaque errors into actionable code fixes, drastically reducing your development cycle.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a terminal full of red text, the cursor blinking like a heartbeat you can’t revive. The excitement of your first SaaS project is rapidly being replaced by the crushing weight of a bug that defies logic. You aren't failing; you've simply reached the inevitable wall every developer hits before they become an engineer.

Relief is closer than you think. By adopting a professional SaaS starter kit, you stop reinventing the wheel and start focusing on the logic that matters. We will use the power of Antigravity to map your architecture and Claude Code to surgically remove technical debt, turning your frustrating debugging sessions into high-velocity learning opportunities.

steps.title: Initialize the Foundation
steps.description: Clone a modern, type-safe SaaS starter kit to ensure your environment includes standardized error logging and structured directory patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project structure and identify the most likely location for a global error boundary to catch runtime exceptions.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Point
steps.description: Use automated tracing to pinpoint exactly where the state logic breaks down before you start guessing at fixes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a sequence diagram showing the data flow between the API route and the database layer for the failing module.

steps.title: Implement Defensive Coding
steps.description: Inject validation layers into your starter kit's data flow to prevent undefined values from crashing your app.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this function to include Zod validation and descriptive error messages for unexpected input types.

steps.title: Automated Remediation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to iterate on the specific function causing the stack trace, keeping the context focused on the fix.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain the logic error in this stack trace and suggest a fix that maintains compatibility with the existing architecture.

steps.title: Verify and Document
steps.description: Ensure the fix is permanent by adding a unit test that specifically targets the edge case you just resolved.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Jest test case that simulates the edge case that caused the bug and verify the fix works.

pro_tips: Always commit your broken state to a new git branch before running AI-assisted fixes to maintain a clean rollback path.
Treat error messages as data, not insults; copy the stack trace exactly into Claude Code to preserve context.
Use Antigravity to visualize dependencies before modifying core files to avoid catastrophic regression bugs.
If a bug persists for more than 30 minutes, step away; your brain is likely ignoring the simplest solution due to fatigue.
Prioritize 'silent' failures by adding custom logging to your starter kit's middleware, allowing you to catch errors before they reach the user.
content.next_steps: Once your first feature is stable, try deploying your app to a staging environment and simulate high-traffic load to uncover race conditions.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the strategy. Now, apply this to your own repository and watch your confidence soar as you squash bugs like a senior dev. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
